texto: Life is too minuscule to waste any proportion of time on wondering what other people think about you. In the introductory place, if they had desirable things going on in their lives, they wouldn't have the time to sit around and talk about you. What's important to me is not others' opinions of me, but what's important to me is my opinion of myself. That is why the forlorn people on this planet, are those who circumspect the most about what other people think.
explicacao: Citação de David Barik sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Life is too minuscule to waste any proportion of time on wondering what other people think about …” articula reflexao, inspiracional, life-lessons em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como inspiracao e life-lessons.

texto: Doesn't matter if you speak either truth or a lie. People are too stubborn to listen only an expected/ pre-concluded/ conjectured answers from you, without having any second thought about the impetus and certitude of the person who speaks. Finally, a cessation that you are no more the same person which you were earlier.
